reasoning: Under the Trump administration, the use of sanctions against Russia has dropped precipitously (only 2 targets designated in 2025 compared to 32 annual packages under Biden). The only recent U.S. sanctions imposed on Russian entities occurred on February 24, 2026, and targeted cybercriminals and IP theft, with no explicit connection to the Ukraine war in the official announcement. Furthermore, recent reports from mid-to-late February 2026 indicate that President Trump is actively stalling a bipartisan sanctions bill in Congress, drawing criticism from Senate Democrats eager to pressure Russia over the war. With only 13 days remaining until the March 14, 2026 deadline and evident executive reluctance to impose new Ukraine-related restrictions, the likelihood of an explicit, war-related sanctions announcement in this brief window is very low.
